claims.statement: Alfabeto chord forms are documented source material for seventeenth-century Spanish five-course guitar accompaniment.
claims.citationLocator: Gaspar Sanz, Instruccion de musica sobre la guitarra espanola (1697 facsimile), Rule 4 and first labyrinth/alfabeto plate, PDF pages 13 and 24

claims.statement: In rasgueado, unnumbered courses sound as part of the chord; a strummed course span therefore cannot silently omit an interior course.
claims.citationLocator: Sanz (1697 facsimile), Rule 4, PDF page 13: courses without numbers in rasgueado are also sounded

claims.statement: Punteado assigns simultaneous and successive attacks to explicit right-hand resources, including the thumb for the bass voice; Vellum's beginner default conservatively limits simultaneous plucked attacks to p-i-m.
claims.citationLocator: Sanz (1697 facsimile), Rules 1-2 for punteado, PDF page 17
